fault_hypothesis: Line 69 uses strict inequalities (`c_end <= start` and `c_start >= end`) which excludes shared endpoints from being considered overlaps. The condition should use strict inequalities `c_end < start` and `c_start > end` so that touching endpoints are treated as overlapping under the closed-interval model.

spec.expr: not (c_end__1 <= start or c_start__1 >= end) == (c_end__1 > start and c_start__1 < end)

constraints.intent: The overlap condition using <= and >= misses boundary touches; the correct check should use strict inequalities so shared endpoints count as overlapping.
When c_end equals start the condition c_end <= start is True, incorrectly skipping an overlapping reservation — a boundary-equal pair should trigger overlap detection.
Confirm that c_end is strictly greater than c_start before it is used in the overlap comparison, ensuring the validated interval is non-degenerate.

spec.expr: (__cbfl_result is True) or all(c_end < s or c_start > e for s, e in reservations)

constraints.intent: If no overlap is returned, every existing reservation must be strictly disjoint from the candidate under the closed-interval model.
